user_query:
Please help me compile an offline lounge plan for my upcoming trip. Read these inputs:
- input/airports.csv for the list of airports (IATA + city)
- input/filters.json for facility requirements
Using only our internal offline lounge catalog (no web lookups), do the following:
1) For each airport in input/airports.csv, list ALL lounges that include every facility in required_facilities from input/filters.json. Treat facility names as exact string matches.
2) Write a consolidated JSON file to output/lounges_by_airport.json with this structure:
   - A JSON object whose keys are airport codes from the input
   - Each key maps to an array of lounge objects, each with fields: id (string), name (string), terminal (string), openingHours (string), facilities (array of strings)
   - Preserve openingHours exactly as in the catalog
3) Also pick ONE "best" lounge per airport from the filtered sets and write a CSV to output/best_picks.csv with header exactly:
   airport_code,lounge_id,lounge_name,terminal,has_showers,reason
   Selection rules (deterministic):
   - Prefer lounges that include all preferred_facilities from input/filters.json
   - If more than one lounge qualifies, pick the one with the lexicographically smallest lounge_name
   - If none include all preferred facilities, pick the lexicographically smallest lounge_name among those meeting required_facilities
   - has_showers must be 'true' if 'Showers' is in that lounge's facilities, else 'false'
   - reason must be exactly one of:
     - "has all required facilities + preferred showers"
     - "has all required facilities (no showers available)"
Airports: please process whatever is in input/airports.csv. For my current data, expect 4 airports. Make sure output paths are exactly output/lounges_by_airport.json and output/best_picks.csv. Do not use any online sources.
